Talisman of Benevolence  
 

Lore Item No Trade Quest Item Placeable
Slot: RANGE SECONDARY
AC: 7
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +15 HP: +38 MANA: +55
SV COLD: +12 SV MAGIC: +12
WT: 0.1 Size: SMALL
Class: CLR DRU SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Talisman of Benevolence
Lore Item No Trade Quest Item Placeable
Slot: RANGE SECONDARY
AC: 7
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +15 HP: +38 MANA: +55
SV COLD: +12 SV MAGIC: +12
WT: 0.1 Size: SMALL
Class: CLR DRU SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Misc
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Divine
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 09:35:51
Page Updated:Fri Nov 8th, 2019

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 50
[Given | Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Leuz's Task

Obtained From

This item is obtained from NPCs.

The Western Wastes
NPC Name Expansion
Scout Charisa Scars of Velious


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
The Western Wastes


Uploaded November 27th, 2008

hey ignoramus. reason we asked is the picture is OLD. not everything gets updated into khazam fekking instantaneously. and yes, the list in text said beastlord, but knowing that many items had been tagged for bst if they were monk/sham/dru useable in khazam just to save time, that WHY i asked.

sheesh.

RE: Beastlord usuable
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2003 at 3:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776013048
View User Forum
263 posts
At one time, Allakhazam had mistakenly flagged druid usable items as beastlord usable. They've corrected most of them, but for a while, this item was listed as usable by beastlords, thus the questions. Seems that it is you who needs to get a clue.

There's no harm in asking.
▲ Page top
Finally
QuoteReply
# Aug 08 2003 at 10:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762552525
11 posts
At long last I completed this quest for the talisman. It was me (a 62 druid), I two boxed a 61 paladin, another 65 druid, 65 cleric, 65chanter, and 65 bard. A bit of overkill but it was a fun fight.

Trust the photo
▲ Page top
RE: beastlords?
QuoteReply
# Mar 31 2003 at 2:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362202595
View User Forum
29 posts
The only problem with trusting the photo is that the photo could have been taken pre-Luclin release and Beastlords wouldn't show up as a possible class able to equip these older pre-Luclin items. Developers had to add Beastlords to many items AFTER Luclin came out...

Don't believe everything you read 'cause someone said it's so =P

Venerable Skratchen Myitchin
60 Vah Shir Savage Lord
▲ Page top
RE: beastlords?
QuoteReply
# May 21 2003 at 7:54 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776013048
View User Forum
263 posts
Didn't mean everytime. In this case, trust the photo.
▲ Page top
Can be done with a non-melee group
QuoteReply
# Dec 11 2002 at 4:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361434542
28 posts
This can be done with 2 lvl 58+ druids, though safer to have three druids and a cleric. Just have both the druids charm an animal in the zone (I prefer the hounds) and use them for the fight.

Tunare's Renewal is needed to keep the hounds healed (why lvl 58+), but a cleric would be even better for the job as I like to re-cast charm to make sure the pet doesn't come back looking for me.

Use the third druid as a back-up to re-charm any pets that break charm.

Edited, Wed Dec 11 15:57:30 2002
▲ Page top
I can confirm...
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2002 at 1:01 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595175391999
17 posts
This quest can be done with amiable faction. I did it tonight at amiable. We did this with 4 melee: 61 SK, 61 Rogue, 59 Pally and 59 SK, a 55 cleric and a 62 druid, with a chanter watching for adds. A shammy to slow might have helped but frankly we clobbered the Captain anyway.

Not too hard at all with the right group.

▲ Page top
RE: Lore
QuoteReply
# Mar 26 2003 at 9:47 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252273128
View User Forum
43 posts
ignore last post, forgot there was a bracer part related to the Talisman
▲ Page top
RE: Lore
QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2002 at 1:48 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591761991555
Scholar
300 posts
If you look at every armor set (set, not unmatched pieces), the bracers are not lore. This includes Kunark, all Velious quest, Planar, newbie, mid-level (like crafted and ivy etched), even the Crystal Chitin out of Velketor's and Rubicite have non-lore bracers. This is because, in a suit of armor, there are two bracers. Everything else has only one (a pair of gloves or boots are considered a single item, and the vambraces are many times connected around the back or over the shoulder to make a single piece.
▲ Page top
RE: Lore
QuoteReply
# Nov 15 2002 at 4:46 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1638145982070
View User Forum
Sage
201 posts
This was not always true. Back in the day alot of those matched armor sets had lore bracers as well. Just a bit of history.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Dec 05 2002 at 1:49 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Yes but they changed it even every newbie armor quest has their bracers as not lore. Get with the times. Doesn't matter what was true in the past. :p
RE: Lore
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2002 at 2:37 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966274592
Scholar
76 posts
if you take notice, alot of armor sets that are lore DONT have lore bracers.

▲ Page top
Talisman is better than Totem for clerics and druids
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2002 at 2:11 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591756918651
99 posts
Wis+18 is worse than Wis+15 with Mana+55, so the Othmir Prexus Totem gives less mana than the Talisman of Benevolence. The Talisman gives better AC and resists, too.

Clerics should shun the Totem in favor of the Talisman, especially since the Talisman is easier to get.

Maybe the Totem should go to Shaman, who can use the extra Stamina.
▲ Page top
RE: Talisman is better than Totem for clerics and druids
QuoteReply
# Jun 22 2002 at 12:33 PM Rating: Decent
Synical
30 posts
Bah, I'd take this; love +12 Save vs. Magic over Stamina any day, and the extra AC is nice too. This is nice for a Cleric OR a Shaman, whichever one did the leg work for the quest first.



Edited, Sat Oct 5 13:50:18 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Talisman is better than Totem for clerics and druids
QuoteReply
# Apr 21 2002 at 2:53 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592342842224
Scholar
110 posts
I'd prefer this over the PKT anyday. Here there is AC, and for me, AC beats out STA anyday

RE: Talisman is better than Totem for clerics and druids
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2002 at 2:05 PM Rating: Default
StrungOut
199 posts

NightGod
85 posts
Regular Fay does drop the druid epic piece and doesn't always drop the sea dragon meat. How do I know?? My guild killed him Sunday the 2nd for one of our druids. Got epic piece, two differnt bracers and five spells.

hes talking about the random spawn one, there are 2 fays now, one randomly spawns, and the other is spawned for the druid epic, they both however drop the item for druid epic, the spawned one drops no loot where the random drops loot and spells, just FYI

60th Druid

RE: Good Lord!
QuoteReply
# Aug 25 2001 at 2:44 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249765599
61 posts
Actually if it's just Wisdom you're looking for, a better rangeslot item is the Othmir Prexus Totem... but that involves killing Faydedar or Kelorek'Dar, both nasty nasty mobs (and Faydedar is triggered too, so you'll have to kill Ulump just to make him spawn) -- at any rate, it's +18 wis Range, which is better than +15... of course I'd still prefer this, due to the many, many other ridiculous bonuses on it.

- R
▲ Page top
RE: Good Lord!

# Jun 25 2002 at 2:54 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361836897
42 posts
As mentioned above, but just to clarify:

The triggered faydaedar drops ONLY the druid epic bit, nothing else, no loot, nothing.


-Amneth
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 10 2001 at 6:30 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Like they said above, the triggered faydedar only drops the piece needed for druid epic. The regulary spawned faydedar drops sea-dragon meat and other junk tho, but no druid epic piece.
